metadata.title:
H|LLYARO READY
TO BECOME


A CITY

pages.raw_text:
Citizens of Hillyard in a mass
meeting presided over by Guy
Seaton last night put the plan of
Incorporating the town in shape
for submission to the county com
missioners in order that an election
date may be fixed. Nominees were
named for the Office of mayor and
treasurer and counciImen. Dr.
Joseph Farrow was nominated for
mayor and John H. Stoneman treas
urer. Seventeen nominations were
made for councilmen.


The preparatory canvass in sup
port of the incorporation movement
has resulted in an estimate OF
1000 population, which is consider
ed safely conservative.


Hillyard citizens claim that they
are receiving no benefit from the
money they pay as county taxes
Their present tax rate is "a mills
which is three mills less than
what they would have to pay as a
part of Spokane.


The Great Northern shops and
yards are excluded from the cor
porate limits proposed, for which
reason it is believed the company
will not block the Incorporation.


The annexation proposition pro
noted by the 10000 club has been
dropped.
